data.intent: a personal finance tracker with envelope budgeting

facade.hypothesis: A friendly, chat-like tone will make financial tracking feel less intimidating and more approachable for personal finance users
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality: friendly vs professional
facade.label: Warm and conversational
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers personality-driven, encouraging language in the interface—consider casual tone in copy, supportive feedback messages, and approachable microcopy
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers a more neutral, professional tone—consider clean, straightforward language without personality, minimal copy, business-like messaging

evidence.hypothesis: A friendly, chat-like tone will make financial tracking feel less intimidating and more approachable for personal finance users
evidence.implication: User prefers personality-driven, encouraging language in the interface—consider casual tone in copy, supportive feedback messages, and approachable microcopy

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gentle, approachable interfaces for managing money rather than rigid or sharp designs
facade.axisTargeted: Roundedness vs. sharpness in shape language
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward warm, friendly, cushioned aesthetic; circular badges for budget categories; rounded input fields; gentle button shapes
facade.rejectImplies: Explore crisp, minimal, or geometric design; sharp edges; clean lines; rectangular cards; structured grid appearance

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer seeing their remaining budget as a single, prominent figure rather than breaking it into category breakdowns
facade.label: One big number
facade.acceptImplies: Show primary metric first; progressive disclosure for detail; metric-first dashboard preferred over itemized lists
facade.rejectImplies: Users need to see all envelope categories at once; granular visibility matters more than simplicity; table or grid view preferred over singular focus

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, animated money movements over instant transfers between envelopes
facade.axisTargeted: Motion continuity vs instant state change
facade.label: Money slides in smoothly
facade.acceptImplies: Build envelope transfers with sliding animations; prioritize visual feedback of money flow; transitions over instant updates
facade.rejectImplies: Keep transfers instant and snappy; minimize animation; prioritize speed and clarity over motion elegance

facade.hypothesis: Users want a gentle, approachable interface that feels less corporate and more like a trusted friend helping with money
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + intimate vs expansive
facade.label: Soft corners & shadows
facade.acceptImplies: Rounded buttons, subtle depth, muted colors, approachable aesthetic — design should feel safe and non-threatening around financial data
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer stark clarity, hard edges, minimal ornamentation, or a more 'serious/professional' money app look — less cozy, more authoritative

facade.hypothesis: Users need persistent budget categories visible while scanning transaction details, suggesting a two-column layout beats full-width tabs
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (sidebar vs tabs) + content density (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick category switching without losing overview; layout should keep envelope list anchored left, transaction cards stacked right
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers focused, single-surface interaction; tabs or sequential paging may work better than parallel column layout

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist/clean aesthetic vs. colorful/visual category representation for envelope budgeting
Whether user wants categories visible at a glance (dashboard grid) vs. nested/hierarchical navigation for budget management
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs. neutral/factual tone when tracking spending
Whether user wants calm/focused atmosphere vs. energetic/urgent atmosphere for financial decisions
Whether user prefers simple summary view vs. detailed transaction-level visibility and reporting
Whether user wants smooth animations and transitions vs. instant/snappy responses when moving money between envelopes

facade.hypothesis: A clean, professional interface with minimal personality will build trust and credibility for financial management, appealing to users who want function over charm
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality — professional vs friendly
facade.label: Serious and minimal
facade.acceptImplies: User values clarity, efficiency, and professional tone in financial tools; minimize casual language, emojis, and playful elements; prioritize data hierarchy and restrained design
facade.rejectImplies: User does not want a sterile or corporate feel; confirm preference for approachable, warmer personality in the interface despite initial hesitation

draft.title: Envelope Budget Tracker
draft.summary: A personal finance app using envelope budgeting to allocate income across spending categories. Users can create envelopes, track spending, and see remaining balance at a glance.
draft.html:
Budget

Manage your envelopes

Total Income

Allocated $2,840
Remaining $660
Envelopes
Groceries
$420 / $500
84% spent $80 left
Dining Out
$180 / $300
60% spent $120 left
Utilities
$240 / $240
100% spent $0 left

draft.title: a personal finance tracker with envelope budgeting
draft.summary: Building your prototype with a warm, conversational tone to make financial tracking feel approachable and less intimidating.
draft.html:
Your Budget, Your Way

Let's build your envelope budget together

Welcome! 👋

Think of envelopes like digital pockets for your money. You decide how much goes into each one—groceries, fun, savings—and we'll help you stay on track without the stress.

Ready to take control of your finances in a way that actually feels good?
draft.nextHint: Next decision: Should the envelope categories be displayed as a colorful grid dashboard or a nested hierarchical list? This will shape the core navigation and visual hierarchy.
